name: Super Duper French Toast
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix eggs, whipping cream, and orange juice in a large casserole or glass baking dish. Add orange liqueur to taste.
2. Remove ends from the bread and cut into 12-15 slices, each about 3/4 inch thick.
3. Place slices in the egg mixture and let stand for a few minutes to absorb some of the liquid. Turn so the other side can also absorb the mixture.
4. Cover tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate until ready to prepare French Toast. Some cooks soak the bread in the egg mixture overnight, refrigerated, making it almost custard-like in texture.
5. Preheat a heavy skillet or electric griddle to 325-350 degrees. Melt butter, margarine, or add oil to the skillet.
6. Place four or five slices in the hot skillet and do not crowd them. Cook on one side until golden brown, then turn and cook on the other side.
7. Remove from heat and keep warm while cooking remaining slices. Add more oil or butter as needed.
8. Place toast on serving plates and dust with powdered sugar. Garnish with fresh fruit if desired and serve with honey, jam, or syrup of your choice. Yields 4-5 servings.
